name: Fecal Microbiota Transplantation
summary: Whole-ecosystem transfer of donor fecal microbiota, usually after screening and sometimes after antibiotic or bowel-preparation conditioning, tracked here for aging-relevant microbiome reset and young-donor replacement strategies.
mechanism_summary: FMT attempts to displace, reseed, or remodel the recipient gut ecosystem by transferring a donor microbial community. Aging-directed versions usually emphasize young or physically active donors, microbiome diversity, SCFA-producing organisms, gut barrier function, inflammaging, metabolic signaling, and gut-brain or gut-muscle axes.

open_questions: Whether young-donor or active-donor microbiota can safely and durably engraft in older adults
Whether microbiome, SCFA, inflammation, metabolic, or frailty biomarker movement translates into functional aging benefit
Which donor-recipient matching, pretreatment, route, dose, and repeat schedule optimize beneficial engraftment
How to control pathogen, inflammatory, metabolic, cancer-risk, or neurobehavioral trait-transfer risks
Whether defined consortia or autologous preserved microbiota can replace crude donor stool for aging uses
